question_text: A bar magnet is cut in half. What will be true about each piece?

options.label: A
options.text: Each piece will have only a north pole or only a south pole

options.label: B
options.text: Each piece will be a complete magnet with its own north and south poles

options.label: C
options.text: The pieces will lose all magnetic properties

options.label: D
options.text: One piece will have two north poles and one will have two south poles

explanation: When a magnet is cut, each piece becomes a new magnet with its own north and south poles. You cannot create a magnet with only one pole; magnetic poles always exist in pairs. This happens because the atoms in each piece still have their magnetic properties aligned.
